item.title: Quantization from the ground up 摘要
item.description:
Sam Rose's interactive essay explains quantization of Large Language Models, covering floating point representation, outlier values crucial for model quality, and accuracy impacts measured via perplexity and KL divergence. Tests on Qwen 3.5 9B show 16-bit to 8-bit quantization has minimal quality loss, while 4-bit retains about 90% accuracy.
标签: quantization, LLM, AI, machine learning, floating point, model optimization, perplexity, Qwen, computer science, interactive tutorial

item.title: Jennifer Daniel on the New ‘Distorted Face’ Emoji 摘要
item.description:
Jennifer Daniel discusses the evolution of emoji, highlighting three recent additions: Melting Face (🫠) representing surrender, Dotted Line Face (🫥) symbolizing sublimation to escape conversations, and the newly proposed Distorted Face (U+1FAEA) depicting physical alteration by emotion. She notes that Apple only includes new emoji in current-year OS updates, so the 8 new emoji are in MacOS 26.4 but not MacOS 15.7.5, despite both being released this week.
标签: emoji, design, technology, Apple, Unicode, operating systems, software updates, digital communication, user experience, trends

item.title: MacOS 26.4 Adds ‘Slow Charger’ Indicator for MacBooks 摘要
item.description:
macOS Tahoe 26.4 introduces a 'Slow Charger' indicator that alerts MacBook users when their charging setup is underpowered. The warning appears in orange text in the battery status menu and settings, with an info button for details. Apple advises using a power adapter and cable that meet the minimum wattage for the MacBook model. This feature is particularly relevant in Europe, where MacBooks no longer include chargers, as users often mistakenly use low-wattage USB-C adapters, leading to battery drain even while plugged in.
标签: macOS, MacBook, battery, charging, power adapter, hardware, Apple, software update, user experience, Europe

item.title: Katie Notopoulos Bids Farewell to Sora: ‘You Were Too Beautiful and Stupid for This World’ 摘要
item.description:
Katie Notopoulos reflects on the short-lived popularity of OpenAI's Sora video generation app, noting that while creating AI videos was initially fun, watching strangers' AI-generated content quickly became boring. She and her friends stopped using the app within weeks, and Meta's similar AI video app also failed to engage users. The article criticizes Sora as an expensive stunt that lacked staying power, highlighting OpenAI's recent struggles as a sign of broader organizational issues.
标签: AI, OpenAI, Sora, video generation, social media, app failure, user engagement, Meta, business strategy, technology trends

item.title: The Information: ‘Apple Can “Distill” Google’s Big Gemini Model’ 摘要
item.description:
Apple has secured extensive access to Google's Gemini AI model under their agreement, allowing Apple to use Gemini in its own data centers to create smaller, specialized models through distillation. This process transfers knowledge from the large Gemini model to smaller ones that can run faster on Apple devices. The deal goes beyond simple white-labeling, as Apple can operate these Gemini-based models in its Private Cloud Compute datacenters, indicating a deep integration.
标签: AI, Apple, Google, Gemini, distillation, machine learning, cloud computing, technology partnership, mobile devices, data centers

item.title: NYT: ‘Melania Trump Appears With a Robot, Saying More Children Should Be Educated by Them’ 摘要
item.description:
The article references a video link showing someone speaking in a particular manner, suggesting an influence on their speech patterns. It also includes a link to a DaringFireball post discussing a New York Times report about Melania Trump appearing with a robot and advocating for more children to be educated by robots, highlighting a connection between public figures and technology in education.
标签: technology, education, robotics, public figures, media, AI, speech patterns, news, social impact, innovation

item.title: Google Brags About Android Web Browser Benchmark Scores on Unnamed Devices; Gullible Reporters Fall for It 摘要
item.description:
Google's Chromium Blog announced that Android is now the fastest mobile platform for web browsing, based on benchmarks Speedometer 3.1 and LoadLine. The claim relies on tests of three unnamed Android flagship phones outperforming an unnamed competing platform (likely iPhone 17 Pro). However, the article criticizes the lack of transparency, as device names and software versions are undisclosed, making verification impossible. It notes that LoadLine, a Google-developed benchmark, is difficult to run on iOS, potentially skewing results. Several tech publications repeated Google's claims without questioning the methodology or anonymity of the devices.
标签: Android, Chrome, benchmark, web performance, mobile, Google, iOS, transparency, tech journalism, Speedometer

item.title: Disney Drops Vaporware $1B Investment in OpenAI After Sora Got Axed 摘要
item.description:
Disney has terminated its partnership with OpenAI, which included a planned $1 billion investment by Disney in the AI company. The decision follows OpenAI's exit from the video generation business and shift in priorities. Disney expressed appreciation for the collaboration and stated it will continue exploring AI platforms to engage fans while respecting intellectual property and creator rights.
标签: AI, business, partnership, investment, media, entertainment, OpenAI, Disney, video generation, corporate strategy

item.title: You Can Jump Right to the Updates Screen in the App Store App on iOS 26.4 摘要
item.description:
The article discusses a user-friendly workaround for accessing the Updates screen in the App Store on iOS 26.4, where the default navigation path was recently made less convenient. It highlights two methods: long-pressing the App Store app icon on the Home Screen to access a contextual menu option for Updates, or creating a Shortcuts shortcut using the URL 'itms-apps://apps.apple.com/updates' to jump directly to the screen. The author prefers the long-press method for its simplicity.
标签: iOS, App Store, updates, shortcuts, user experience, mobile, Apple, tips, navigation, productivity

item.title: Lebesgue constants 摘要
item.description:
This article explains Lebesgue constants in numerical interpolation, focusing on the constant λ that amplifies errors in tabulated values. It defines the Lebesgue function and constant based on interpolation nodes, provides asymptotic expressions for evenly spaced and Chebyshev-spaced grids, and illustrates with examples: for n=11 and n=29, evenly spaced grids amplify errors by factors of 155 and over 10 million, while Chebyshev spacing yields much lower factors of 2.58 and 3.17, highlighting its superiority for high-order interpolation.
标签: numerical analysis, interpolation, Lebesgue constants, error analysis, Chebyshev polynomials, mathematics, computational methods, asymptotic expressions, grid spacing, high-order interpolation

item.title: The Apple Charging Situation 摘要
item.description:
The article discusses Apple's charging ecosystem, referencing a Daring Fireball link about macOS 26.4 slow charger warnings and an information guide from Rands in Repose. It highlights issues with power adapters, particularly slow charging warnings in newer macOS versions, and provides useful insights into the broader Apple charging situation, including compatibility and user experience concerns.
标签: Apple, macOS, charging, power adapters, hardware, user experience, technology, guides, Rands in Repose, Daring Fireball

item.title: Members Only: On Cathedral thinking 摘要
